channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: France's foreign minister is in China to discuss Ukraine and trade issues
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot began a two-day visit to China on Thursday during which he is to hold talks with his Chinese counterpart on Ukraine and longstanding trade disputes between China and Europe.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Police use force to break up protests at university in Turkey's capital
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — Police used pepper spray, plastic pellets and water cannon against protesters in Turkey's capital early Thursday, potentially reigniting tensions after two days of relative calm in the country's biggest anti-government protests in over a decade.
item.dc:creator: ROBERT BADENDIECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NATO clarifies comments that four missing US soldiers had died during training in Lithuania
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — NATO on Wednesday clarified comments that Secretary-General Mark Rutte made earlier in the day, when he suggested that four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania had died, even though the U.S. Army said their fate was not yet confirmed.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazil ex-President Bolsonaro will stand trial over an alleged coup plan. Here’s what happens next
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — A panel of Brazil Supreme Court justices unanimously ruled that former President Jair Bolsonaro and seven of his associates will stand trial on five counts, including attempting to stage a coup after the far-right leader lost the 2022 election.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Signal leak: Trump adviser Mike Waltz may have to go, Warner says
item.description: “There needs to be accountability,” said Sen. Warner, who faults Trump's inner circle with a number of security gaffes.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea sent 3,000 more troops to Russia, according to South's assessment
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea sent around 3,000 additional troops to Russia in January and February in continued support for Russian President Vladimir Putin’s war on Ukraine, South Korea’s military said Thursday in its latest assessment.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Palestinians protest Hamas in a rare public show of dissent in Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Thousands of Palestinians marched between the wreckage of a heavily destroyed town in northern Gaza on Wednesday in the second day of anti-war protests, with many chanting against Hamas in a rare display of public anger against the militant group.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, FATMA KHALED and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea’s centuries-old Gounsa temple is left in ruins by unprecedented wildfires
item.description: Wildfires in South Korea's southern regions have left 24 people dead and destroyed more than 300 structures, including large parts of an ancient Buddhist temple complex.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump shifts Greenland approach amid blowback, Signal scandal
item.description: What looked like a Trump administration charm offensive in its gambit to take over Greenland, billed as a cultural heritage tour led by second lady Usha Vance, has turned into something different with the inclusion of her husband and the explosion of a national security scandal at home.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Family unknowingly had rare, valuable painting for 150 years
item.description: A painting hanging in a French family's living room turned out to be far more valuable than just another trinket or heirloom.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sudan’s army chief returns to Khartoum for first time in 2 years of war after airport recaptured
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Sudan's army said Wednesday it had recaptured Khartoum's international airport, and the military chief flew back to the capital for the first time in nearly two years of war, bringing the military closer to regaining full control of the city from the rival Rapid Support Forces paramilit
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel orders more evacuations and Palestinians stage rare anti-Hamas protests
item.description: The Israeli military on Wednesday ordered the evacuation of parts of Gaza City as it steps up its renewed offensive against Hamas after breaking the ceasefire last week.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missing US soldiers in Lithuania killed: NATO chief
item.description: Four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania have died, NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said Wednesday. Speaking to reporters during a trip to Warsaw, Rutte said he had received word of the deaths of the four American troops.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Sudanese army surrounds Khartoum airport in battle for capital, military sources say
item.description: The Sudanese army is encircling Khartoum airport as it battles to oust its rival Rapid Support Forces from its final foothold in Sudan's capital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brazilian Supreme Court panel orders ex-President Bolsonaro to stand trial over alleged coup plan
item.description: A panel of Brazil Supreme Court justices has unanimously accepted charges against former President Jair Bolsonaro over an alleged attempt to stay in office after his 2022 election defeat.
item.dc:creator: Eléonore Hughes, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: His brother’s keeper: Ilay David warns his brother, Hamas hostage Evyatar, is running out of time
item.description: Evyatar David's brother, Ilay, is fighting just to play music together again. For almost a year and a half, Evyatar has been held hostage in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: German authorities raid group that allegedly aims to overthrow Eritrean government
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Police were executing raids in six German states Wednesday against a group that authorities say “is part of an international network whose aim is to overthrow the government in Eritrea.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cruise passengers issued warning while passing through pirate hot spot
item.description: Passengers aboard the "Queen Anne" ship were issued an alarming warning while passing through a pirate hot spot.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: UN to reduce staff in Gaza, blaming Israel for a strike that killed its employee
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The United Nations said Monday it will “reduce its footprint” in the Gaza Strip after an Israeli tank strike hit one of its compounds last week, killing one staffer from Bulgaria and wounding five other employees.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, EDITH M. LEDERER and LEE KEATH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes kill scores in Gaza as Egypt offers new ceasefire proposal
item.description: Israeli strikes across the Gaza Strip killed more than 65 Palestinians over the past day, including women and children, the Gaza Health Ministry said Monday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: South Korean PM Han Duck-soo reinstated as acting president after impeachment overturned
item.description: South Korea's Constitutional Court overturned acting President Han Duck-soo's impeachment, reinstating the nation's No. 2 official as acting leader on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tourist arrested, attacked by locals after scaling world-famous sacred Mayan temple, video shows
item.description: Video shows a tourist scaling the steps of Mexico's Temple of Kukulcan as thousands of visitors flocked to the site to witness the spring equinox last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis is back home after a 5-week hospital stay for life-threatening double pneumonia
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — A frail Pope Francis returned to the Vatican on Sunday after a five-week hospitalization for life-threatening double pneumonia, and he made a surprise stop at his favorite basilica on the way home before beginning two months of prescribed rest and recovery.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, PAOLO SANTALUCIA and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: New Canadian prime minister calls snap election to respond to Trump
item.description: New Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney kicked off his election campaign on Sunday ahead of the country's 45th federal election scheduled on April 28.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Christian watch group rises up to protect community amid growing violence in Syria
item.description: The Eagles of Antioch is a group of volunteers who stand watch over Christian neighborhoods in Damascus, protecting shrines, icons and churches from vandalism and attacks in post-Assad Syria.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: America's European allies try to pry unspent money back from USAID
item.description: Three European allies provided millions of dollars that the United States was supposed to spend for low-income countries.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, JAMEY KEATEN, MIKE CORDER AND VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: European countries warn against US travel amid immigration crackdown
item.description: The UK and Germany updated travel advisories after citizens were arrested and detained when attempting to enter the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Israel returns to war in Gaza with wider aims and almost no constraints
item.description: Israel's renewed military offensive in the Gaza Strip threatens to be even deadlier and more destructive than the last, as it pursues wider aims with far fewer constraints.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Finland is again ranked the happiest country in the world. The US falls to its lowest-ever position
item.description: HELSINKI (AP) — Finland is the happiest country in the world for the eighth year in a row, according to the World Happiness Report 2025 published Thursday.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER and KOSTYA MANENKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9
